red_baseline.reason: T015 owns the failure-first canonical clean plan, digest drift, protected evidence, unsafe path, journal, reconciliation, and CLI compatibility contract; T016 owns implementation and CLI migration.
red_baseline.summary: Final RED: risky clean and forbidden confirmation-shaped inputs pass; preview/apply/mapping assertions fail only on missing T016 cutover, and both Kernel files fail only because the T016-owned clean handler module is absent.

validation.summary: All three safe-clean contract files parse successfully.

1/6 discovered units passes the risky/confirmation exclusion boundary; three real CLI cases fail only on missing planDigest/Agent mapping, and two Kernel files fail only on missing src/kernel/operations/clean.js.
Syntax check passed for 83 JavaScript files.
All T015 contract and lifecycle changes passed whitespace validation.

review.evidence: The RED contract freezes canonical order-independent plans, lock-held recomputation, digest drift refusal before deletion, root/outside/symlink refusal, protected evidence propagation, prepared/running fault windows, evidence-only recovery, schemaVersion 1 CLI mirrors, and explicit exclusion of risky/confirmation-shaped Agent authority.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Tests expose only configured local safe targets; risky clean remains CLI-only and public Agent requests cannot nominate paths or risky targets.
Fault contracts require durable prepared/running evidence, one apply effect, and reconciliation of the original operation without replay.
Only clean.plan and digest-bound clean.applySafe are tested as public routes; clean --all --yes remains outside Agent results.
The stale-digest contract requires recomputation while mutation locks are held, plan_digest_mismatch, zero apply calls, effects none, and a refused journal record.
A matching digest authorizes only the already public safe operation; it cannot authorize risky targets or replace human confirmation for CLI-only clean --all.
yes, confirmed, approvalToken, riskyTargets, path, and nested confirmation-shaped fields are all rejected by the closed clean.applySafe input contract.
Fault tests require prepared persistence before revalidation/deletion, running persistence before the first apply effect, and retained evidence for post-effect recovery.
